fields.title: About Open Journals Collective
fields.body:
Who we are

Open Journals Collective (OJC) is a not-for-profit organisation dedicated to supporting the transition to open access publishing across academia. Founded by a coalition of librarians, researchers, and publishers, we believe that knowledge should be freely available to all.

Our mission

We exist to make high-quality peer-reviewed research accessible without financial barriers. Our work spans three core areas:

  • Supporting journals in transitioning to open access models
  • Advocating for sustainable funding mechanisms that replace subscription revenue
  • Building a community of practice around open scholarship

Our values

We believe the results of publicly funded research should be publicly available. Full stop.

This principle underpins everything we do. We work collaboratively with publishers, institutions, and funders to build systems that serve the academic community rather than extract value from it.

Governance

OJC is governed by an elected board drawn from our member institutions. Board meetings are held quarterly and minutes are published openly. Our finances are audited annually and accounts are available to all members.

RoleNameInstitution
ChairProf. Sarah MensahUniversity of Edinburgh
Deputy ChairDr. Ravi PatelImperial College London
TreasurerDr. Anke BauerHumboldt-Universität zu Berlin
SecretaryDr. Lucia FerreiraUniversidade de São Paulo

History

OJC was established in 2019 following a series of workshops at the Berlin Declaration on Open Access. What began as an informal network of journal editors has grown into a formal organisation with over 200 member institutions across 40 countries.

fields.meta_description_en: Learn about Open Journals Collective — our mission, values, governance, and history as a not-for-profit open access organisation.

fields.title: Join the Movement
fields.body:
Why join OJC?

Membership of OJC gives your institution a voice in shaping the future of open access publishing. Whether you are a university library, a research funder, or an independent journal, there is a membership tier designed for you.

Membership tiers

  1. Founding Member — for institutions that have supported OJC from the beginning. Founding members have permanent seats on the advisory council and discounted rates for all OJC services.
  2. Full Member — open to any academic institution, funder, or publisher. Full members vote in board elections and access the full OJC service portfolio.
  3. Associate Member — for smaller institutions and independent journals. Associate members receive all benefits except voting rights.
  4. Supporter — for individuals and organisations who wish to signal solidarity without formal membership commitments.

What membership includes

  • Access to OJC's journal transition support programme
  • Discounted Article Processing Charges (APCs) for authors at member institutions
  • Participation in the OJC community forum and annual conference
  • Use of the OJC quality mark on qualifying journals
  • Monthly briefings on open access policy developments

Fees

TierAnnual feeVoting rights
Founding Member£2,500Yes
Full Member£1,500Yes
Associate Member£500No
Supporter£50No

Membership fees are reinvested entirely into OJC's programmes. We have no shareholders and pay no dividends.

How to apply

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. To begin, email membership@ojc.org with your institution's name, type, and preferred membership tier. Our team will respond within five working days.

fields.title: Our Journal Standards
fields.body:
The OJC quality mark

Journals that meet OJC's editorial and ethical standards are eligible to display the OJC quality mark. This signals to readers, authors, and indexers that the journal operates to the highest standards of open scholarship.

Editorial standards

To qualify, journals must demonstrate compliance across four areas:

Peer review

  • All research articles must undergo double-blind peer review by at least two independent referees
  • Review timelines must be disclosed to authors at submission
  • Reviewer conflicts of interest must be declared and managed

Editorial independence

  • Editorial decisions must be made free from advertiser or funder influence
  • Editors must declare any financial interests in submitted work
  • Retraction and correction policies must be published and followed

Transparency

  • Ownership and funding sources must be disclosed on the journal website
  • Article Processing Charge (APC) waiver policies must be published
  • Acceptance rates must be reported annually

Preservation

  • All content must be deposited with at least one recognised digital preservation service (e.g. CLOCKSS, Portico)
  • DOIs must be assigned to all articles
  • ORCID integration is strongly recommended

Assessment process

Applications are assessed by OJC's Standards Committee, a panel of twelve independent academics and librarians. Assessment takes approximately eight weeks. Journals are reassessed every three years.

We do not charge for assessment. The quality mark is available to any journal that meets our standards, regardless of size or resources.

Standards document

The full OJC Standards document (v2.4, January 2026) is available as a PDF download. Comments and proposed revisions can be submitted via the standards consultation portal.

fields.title: Contact Us
fields.body:
Get in touch

We welcome enquiries from journals, institutions, researchers, and anyone with an interest in open access publishing. Please use the relevant contact below to ensure your message reaches the right team.

General enquiries

Email: hello@ojc.org
We aim to respond to all general enquiries within three working days.

Membership

Email: membership@ojc.org
For questions about joining OJC, renewing membership, or changing your tier.

Standards and quality mark

Email: standards@ojc.org
For enquiries about applying for the OJC quality mark or the standards assessment process.

Press and media

Email: press@ojc.org
For interview requests, comment, or press materials. Our communications team is available Monday to Friday, 09:00–17:00 GMT.

Registered address

Open Journals Collective
c/o Edinburgh University Press
The Tun — Holyrood Road
Edinburgh
EH8 8PJ
United Kingdom

OJC does not have a public reception. Please contact us by email before visiting our registered address.

Report a concern

If you have a concern about a journal that displays the OJC quality mark — including suspected misconduct, undisclosed conflicts of interest, or failure to follow retraction policies — please email integrity@ojc.org. All reports are treated confidentially.

fields.title: Privacy Policy
fields.body:
Last updated: 1 January 2026

1. Who we are

Open Journals Collective (OJC, we, us) is a not-for-profit organisation registered in Scotland (SC654321). We are the data controller for personal data processed in connection with our website and services.

2. What data we collect

We collect the following categories of personal data:

  • Contact information — name, email address, and institutional affiliation provided when you contact us or apply for membership
  • Account data — username and password for users of the OJC member portal
  • Usage data — IP addresses, browser type, and pages visited, collected automatically via server logs
  • Payment data — processed by our payment provider (Stripe); we do not store card details

3. How we use your data

PurposeLegal basis
Responding to enquiriesLegitimate interests
Processing membership applicationsContract performance
Sending member communicationsLegitimate interests / Consent
Improving our websiteLegitimate interests
Complying with legal obligationsLegal obligation

4. Data retention

We retain contact data for as long as necessary to fulfil the purpose for which it was collected, or as required by law. Member account data is retained for the duration of membership plus two years. Usage logs are retained for 90 days.

5. Your rights

Under UK GDPR, you have the right to:

  1. Access the personal data we hold about you
  2. Rectify inaccurate data
  3. Erase your data (subject to legal retention obligations)
  4. Object to processing based on legitimate interests
  5. Data portability
  6. Lodge a complaint with the ICO (ico.org.uk)

To exercise any of these rights, email privacy@ojc.org.

6. Cookies

We use only essential session cookies required for the operation of our member portal. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.

7. Changes to this policy

We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated to members by email. The current version is always available at this URL.

fields.title: Annual Conference 2026
fields.body:
OJC Annual Conference — Edinburgh, June 2026

The OJC Annual Conference brings together open access advocates, journal editors, librarians, and policymakers for two days of talks, workshops, and debate. The 2026 edition will be held at the University of Edinburgh's Informatics Forum.

Programme highlights

Day one — Wednesday 17 June

  • 09:00 — Registration and coffee
  • 10:00 — Opening keynote: The next decade of open access — Prof. Sarah Mensah
  • 11:30 — Panel: Transformative agreements — progress or distraction?
  • 14:00 — Workshop stream A: Building a diamond open access journal
  • 14:00 — Workshop stream B: Metadata standards for open scholarship
  • 17:00 — Networking reception, sponsored by Edinburgh University Press

Day two — Thursday 18 June

  • 09:30 — Keynote: Artificial intelligence and peer review — Dr. Yuki Tanaka, Kyoto University
  • 11:00 — Members' forum and AGM
  • 14:00 — Workshop stream C: APC waivers in practice
  • 14:00 — Workshop stream D: Inclusive publishing for the Global South
  • 16:00 — Closing remarks and 2027 conference announcement

Registration

Ticket typeEarly bird (before 31 March)Standard
Full member£195£250
Associate member£245£310
Non-member£395£495
Student / unwaged£50£50

A limited number of bursaries are available for delegates from low- and middle-income countries. Apply by 28 February via the bursary application form.

Venue and travel

The Informatics Forum is located at 10 Crichton Street, Edinburgh EH8 9AB, a five-minute walk from Edinburgh Waverley station. A block of rooms has been reserved at the Holiday Inn Royal Mile at a rate of £135 per night — use code OJC2026 when booking.

Call for proposals

We are accepting proposals for workshop sessions and lightning talks until 31 January 2026. Submissions should be no more than 400 words and include a brief biography of the presenter. Send proposals to conference@ojc.org.
